golf.result: McIlroy wins 2026 Masters at -12 · Scheffler 2nd · Cameron Young T3 · Historic back-to-back
golf.note: 4th player ever to defend at Augusta: Nicklaus, Faldo, Tiger, McIlroy

nascar.result: Ty Gibbs wins Food City 500 at Bristol OT — 0.055s over Blaney · Gibbs' 1st Cup win
nascar.note: Closest Bristol finish since Wallace/Irvan 1991 · Reddick still leads Cup standings

topStories.headline: Rory McIlroy joins Nicklaus, Faldo and Tiger — the back-to-back Masters champion
body: After squandering a six-shot lead on Saturday, Rory McIlroy dug deep on Sunday and did what only three men before him had ever done — won consecutive Masters titles. He birdied holes 7, 8, 12, and 13 to retake the lead as Cameron Young and Justin Rose both made costly errors in the final nine holes. McIlroy finished at –12, one shot clear of Scottie Scheffler, who was the day's most relentless chaser with a closing 67.

McIlroy now joins Jack Nicklaus (1965–66), Nick Faldo (1989–90), and Tiger Woods (2001–02) as the only players to defend the Masters title. He has four major championships and looks as complete as he has ever been.

topStories.headline: PSG eliminate Liverpool 4–0 on aggregate — and Atlético stun Barcelona on away goals
body: Ousmane Dembélé scored twice at Anfield — 72nd minute and stoppage time — as PSG shut Liverpool out at their own ground and advanced 4–0 on aggregate. Liverpool's Champions League campaign is over. PSG are now one of the clear favorites to win the competition.

In Madrid, Barcelona won 2–1 at the Metropolitano — Lamine Yamal and Ferran Torres scored inside 24 minutes — but it wasn't enough. Atlético's Ademola Lookman pulled one back to make it 3–2 on aggregate, and Atlético advance. It's a brutal exit for Barcelona, who led in the tie and still went out.

topStories.headline: LaMelo's layup, Bridges' block — Hornets eliminate Miami in overtime heartbreak
body: LaMelo Ball hit a go-ahead layup with seconds left, then Miles Bridges swatted away the Heat's final attempt as Charlotte won 127–126 in overtime to eliminate Miami from the play-in tournament. The Heat — a team that's made deep playoff runs in recent years — are done. In the West, the Trail Blazers beat Phoenix 114–110 to claim the 7-seed. Tonight: Magic vs. 76ers (7:30 ET) and Warriors vs. Clippers (10 ET) decide the final two spots.

stories.headline: Trail Blazers are in — Portland's improbable run continues into the first round
body: The Portland Trail Blazers are a playoff team. Their 114–110 win over Phoenix in the play-in earned the West's 7-seed, setting up a first-round matchup that will be announced once tonight's games conclude. Portland has been one of the league's best stories in the second half of the season — their depth and Jerami Grant's clutch shooting have made them dangerous.

stories.headline: Magic vs. 76ers: Embiid back healthy against a young Orlando squad
body: Joel Embiid returns at full strength for Philadelphia after nursing an oblique injury all season. The Sixers (7-seed) host the Magic (8-seed) at 7:30 ET on Prime Video. Paolo Banchero and Orlando's young core have played fearless basketball all year. The winner faces the No. 2 seed in the first round. In the West, Golden State vs. LA Clippers (10 ET) is a potential Steph Curry farewell game if the Warriors lose.

stories.headline: Flyers clinch, Hurricanes lock up the East — the bracket is set
body: The Philadelphia Flyers clinched a playoff spot with a shootout win over Carolina on Monday — Tyson Foerster scored the decisive attempt. Carolina, meanwhile, clinched the Eastern Conference's top seed. The Dallas Stars saw Rémi Bourque score his first NHL hat trick with three goals in the third period to beat Toronto. The St. Louis Blues mounted a 5-goal rally to down Minnesota 6–3. The regular season ends Thursday, playoffs begin Saturday April 19.

stories.headline: Angels hand Yankees their worst loss — early stumble for New York
body: The Los Angeles Angels dealt the Yankees a 7–1 beatdown on Tuesday — a sharp reality check for a New York team that started 8–2. The Yankees' vaunted pitching ran into a hot Angels lineup, and it wasn't competitive. The Dodgers (11–3) remain the NL's best record through three weeks, with Shohei Ohtani's on-base streak now at 41 games.

stories.headline: Bayern–Real Madrid at the Allianz Arena — Kane vs. Vinícius, winner takes all
body: Real Madrid must win in Munich tonight to advance — a loss or draw knocks them out as Bayern lead 2–1 on aggregate. Vinícius Júnior will carry Madrid's hopes; he scored both goals in the first leg. Harry Kane has been in unstoppable form all season and will front Bayern's attack on home soil. The Allianz Arena hasn't been beaten in the UCL all season. This is the tie of the round — kickoff 3 ET on Paramount+.

stories.headline: Arsenal at home vs Sporting — Havertz and Saka should finish the job
body: Arsenal hold a 1–0 lead from the first leg and host Sporting CP tonight. The Gunners have been outstanding defensively and Kai Havertz has been their UCL clutch performer. A clean sheet tonight would advance them to the semis in style. The UCL semi-final draw will take place immediately after tonight's results, setting up the final four.

stories.headline: Antonelli's championship lead — Miami marks the next chapter in the 2026 title fight
body: Kimi Antonelli leads George Russell by 9 points in the championship after three races. Both drives for Mercedes have won races, making it an intra-team title fight for the ages. Charles Leclerc trails but Ferrari has been improving race by race. The Miami Grand Prix (May 1–3) is next — and the Florida heat typically shuffles the order.

stories.headline: Monte Carlo Masters underway — Sinner's clay debut expected Wednesday or Thursday
body: The Monte Carlo Masters is in its early rounds this week. Jannik Sinner, heavy favorite coming off his Sunshine Double, is expected to make his first-round appearance Wednesday or Thursday. Carlos Alcaraz, who has won Roland Garros three times, is the primary threat on clay. Early upsets are already being tracked on the red clay of the Principality — the clay season's opening major salvo ahead of the French Open on May 25.

stories.headline: The Sunday collapse that almost cost him — and the four birdies that saved him
body: The full narrative of McIlroy's second Masters: blow a record 36-hole lead on Saturday, wake up co-leading on Sunday, survive a final round that saw both Cameron Young and Justin Rose hold two-shot leads at points, and birdie 12 and 13 to retake control for good. Scottie Scheffler closed brilliantly with 67 to finish solo second — but McIlroy was just better when it mattered. It may be the most dramatic final round Augusta has seen in years.

stories.headline: Ty Gibbs ends 131-race wait — the closest Bristol finish since 1991
body: Ty Gibbs had gone 131 Cup Series starts without a win. On Sunday at Bristol, he held off Ryan Blaney by 0.055 seconds in overtime to finally get it done. Kyle Larson finished third. It was the closest finish at Bristol since Rusty Wallace beat Ernie Irvan by a foot in 1991. Gibbs races for Joe Gibbs Racing — his grandfather's team — making the moment deeply personal. Tyler Reddick still leads the Cup standings overall with four wins.
